summary: Tested Aether auth + Stripe + docs feature. Backend pytest: 14/16 pass. Frontend: all flows pass (unauth redirect, login/signup, admin dashboard with 5000 credits + FOUNDING BUILDER, user profile popover, BuyCreditsModal with 6 tiers triggering POST /api/stripe/checkout 200, docs hub + article markdown rendering, /terms /privacy /payment routes, sign out). Two backend bugs found: (1) brute-force lockout broken behind k8s ingress, (2) /api/stripe/status returns 500.

critical.issue: Brute-force lockout never triggers behind k8s ingress. identifier = f'{request.client.host}:{email}' and request.client.host returns the upstream pod IP, which varies between calls (observed 10.231.130.130 and 10.231.129.113 across consecutive POSTs from same test session). Result: 6 failed attempts on same email returned 401, 401, 401, 401, 401, 401 — never 429. Fix: derive identifier from X-Forwarded-For (first hop) or use email-only as the brute-force key (with optional IP as secondary).

Returns HTTP 500 with CheckoutError 'No such checkout.session: cs_test_...'. POST /api/stripe/checkout creates a session via the Emergent Stripe proxy (https://integrations.emergentagent.com/stripe/v1/checkout/sessions) and returns a real Stripe-style session_id, but emergentintegrations.StripeCheckout.get_checkout_status appears to query api.stripe.com directly, which does not know about that session. Either (a) get_checkout_status must also route through the Emergent proxy, or (b) wrap the call in try/except and surface a 502 with meaningful message instead of bare 500. /payment/success polling will fail silently in production.

issues: Bottom footer text 'TERMS OF SERVI...' and 'PRIVACY POLICY' partially clipped at 1920px viewport — long uppercase letter-spacing tracking pushes text beyond container. Not critical but cosmetically poor.

action_items: Fix brute-force lockout identifier to be load-balancer-safe (use X-Forwarded-For first hop or pure email)
Fix /api/stripe/status to route status fetch through Emergent Stripe proxy (or catch CheckoutError and return 502 with descriptive payload)
Adjust sidebar footer text width / tracking so 'Terms of Service' and 'Privacy Policy' fit at 1920px
critical_code_review_comments: server.py line 266: ip = request.client.host — behind any reverse proxy this is unreliable. Use request.headers.get('x-forwarded-for', '').split(',')[0].strip() or trust X-Real-IP.
server.py line 439: sc.get_checkout_status(session_id) — no try/except. Any SDK error escapes as raw 500. Wrap and return controlled error.

server.py: no automatic refresh-token rotation endpoint exists despite refresh_token cookie being set on login/signup. Either implement /auth/refresh or document that the 1-day access_token is the de-facto session lifetime.

server.py public_user(): the conditional for member_since is fragile and may produce non-ISO strings if created_at is a string already that's not ISO — consider always storing datetime objects in mongo (motor handles BSON dates).
BuyCreditsModal.jsx line 47: window.location.href = data.url assumes data.url exists; no validation. Add fallback if Stripe checkout returns empty url.
BuyCreditsModal.jsx: PACKS/PLANS hard-coded on the frontend duplicate the source-of-truth PRICING_TIERS on the backend. Should fetch /api/stripe/pricing once and render dynamically to prevent drift.
test_credentials: admin@aether.dev / aether_admin_2026 — works. Signup creates users with credits=100, plan=free.
seed_data_creation: Tests create ephemeral TEST_/uuid users via /api/auth/signup. No cleanup performed (no admin delete endpoint).
context_for_next_testing_agent: All UI flows verified working. Two backend issues to verify after main agent fix: (1) lockout via X-Forwarded-For — run TestBruteForce::test_lockout_after_failures, (2) Stripe status — run TestStripeCheckout::test_status_own_session. Use admin@aether.dev / aether_admin_2026. /api/stripe/checkout returns real test-mode Stripe URL successfully; redirect was confirmed by intercepting checkout.stripe.com navigation.
rca_of_the_issue: 1) lockout: identifier built from request.client.host which in k8s/ingress contexts is the upstream pod IP, not the client IP. Different pods serve different requests in a session → counter never reaches 5 for same identifier. 2) stripe/status 500: session created via emergent proxy is unknown to real Stripe API; SDK's get_checkout_status hits api.stripe.com (per error req_llObON2y... and 'No such checkout.session'). The create_checkout_session uses integrations.emergentagent.com but status path appears to bypass proxy.
